coredata.dc:title: A ratiometric fluorescence method for detection of Fe3+ based on longer wavelength excitation luminescence of homovanillic acid
coredata.dc:creator: Zhang, Hui
Liu, Xingcen
Sun, Jia
Wu, Xia
coredata.dc:description: A novel luminescence phenomenon of homovanillic acid (HVA) that the excitation wavelength is longer than the emission wavelength (LExL, λex-L > λem) was observed, which was monitored by using a common spectrofluorometer with a xenon lamp as an excitation light source. As the addition of Fe3+, the intrinsic fluorescence of HVA at 310 nm was obviously quenched and a new emission peak at 424 nm emerged and raised under 557 nm excitation. The similar emission spectra of HVA-Fe3+ were also obtained at 287 nm excitation. Thus, a dual-mode ratiometric fluorescence (RF) method for detecting Fe3+ was developed based on LExL and Stokes fluorescence (SF) of HVA. The RF signals of lg(I 424 nm/I 310 nm) had excellent linearity with Fe3+ concentration in the range of 0.50–100 μM. Their detection limits were 0.073 μM and 0.090 μM for the LExL and SF methods, respectively. The LExL and SF methods exhibited high selectivity for detecting Fe3+, and the former has stronger anti-interference ability to Cu2+, Fe2+ etc. The proposed two methods were successfully applied for the determination of Fe3+ in actual water samples. The interaction mechanism of HVA with Fe3+ was also studied.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Ratiometric fluorescence
Multi-signal recognition
Homovanillic acid
Iron ion

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Collection and pre-treatment of real water samples
Fluorescence and absorption characteristics of HVA
Effects of experimental conditions for the detection of Fe3+
Ratiometric fluorescence for the detection of Fe3+

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: National Natural Science Foundation of China
Natural Science Foundation of Shandong Province

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: 3D fluorescence spectra of (a) LExL and (b) SF of HVA; 3D fluorescence spectra of (c) LExL and (d) SF of HVA-Fe3+. Conditions: HVA: 10 μM, Fe3+:70 μM, citric acid-Na2HPO4 buffer solution: 5.0 mM (pH = 6.00). The contour intervals of LExL and SF are 2 nm and 200 nm, respectively.

(a) Fluorescence intensity ratio (I 424 nm/I 310 nm) of HVA upon addition of various metal ions. Inset: fluorescence spectra of LExL and SF of HVA with various metal ions, and photographs of the aqueous HVA (100 μM) containing various metal ions taken under 302 nm UV light. (b) Relative fluorescence intensity ratio (I 424 nm/I 310 nm) of HVA-Fe3+ mixture with various metal ions. Conditions: HVA: 10 μM, metal ions: 100 μM (Ba2+, Al3+, Zn2+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Cr3+, Co2+, Ni2+, Cd2+, Pb2+, Hg2+, Ag+, Fe3+, Fe2+, Cu2+), citric acid-Na2HPO4 buffer solution: 5.0 mM (pH = 6.00).

Fluorescence spectra and linear relationships of (a) LExL and (b) SF of HVA in response to Fe3+. Conditions: HVA: 10 μM, Fe3+: 0, 0.50, 0.70, 1.0, 3.0, 5.0, 10, 30, 50, 70, 100 μM, citric acid-Na2HPO4 buffer solution: 5.0 mM (pH = 6.00).

(a) UV-vis absorption spectra of the HVA-Fe3+ system. Conditions: HVA: 10 μM, Fe3+: 5.0 μM, citric acid-Na2HPO4 buffer solution: 5.0 mM (pH = 6.00). Buffer solution as the blank. (b) FT-IR spectra of HVA and HVA-Fe3+.
Schematic diagram of interaction mechanism of HVA with Fe3+.
Comparison of some reported ratiometric fluorescent method and the proposed method in this study for the detection of Fe3+.

ce:table-footnote.ce:label: a
ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: Compound L was synthesized by 4-morpholin-1,8-naphthalic anhydride, p-phenylenediamine and RhodamineB.

ce:table-footnote.ce:label: b
ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: 2-(1-Pyrenyl) benzimidazole was synthesized by Pyrene-1-carboxaldehyde and o-phenylenediamine.

ce:table-footnote.ce:label: c
ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: functionalized MOF was Eu2(OH-BDC)3 (OH-BDC = 2-hydroxyterephthalic acid) and was synthesized via in situ ligand formation.

ce:table-footnote.ce:label: d
ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: CDs were prepared with anhydrous citric acid and diethylenetriamine.

ce:table-footnote.ce:label: e
ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: UCNPs-EPA was the composites of NaYF4: Gd Yb, Ho upconversion nanoparticles and N,N-diethyl-p-phenylenediamine.

ce:table-footnote.ce:label: f
ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: DCDs was dual-emission carbon dots (DCDs) and was synthesized by carbonizing glutathione in the water/formamide mixture.

ce:table-footnote.ce:label: g
ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: RDDFT was 4-Hydroxy-5-isopropyl-2-methylisophthalaldehyde anchored rhodamine B derivative.

ce:table-footnote.ce:label: h
ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: Dual-emission CDs consist of the blue carbon dots (b-CDs) and the yellow carbon dots (y-CDs). b-CDs were synthesized with glutathione and sodium citrate, and y-CDs were synthesized with 1,2,4-triaminobenzene.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Results of the detection of Fe3+ in water sample of Daming Lake (n = 3).

head.ce:title: A ratiometric fluorescence method for detection of Fe3+ based on longer wavelength excitation luminescence of homovanillic acid

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Shandong University, Key Laboratory of Colloid and Interface Chemistry (Shandong University), Ministry of Education, Jinan 250100, People’s Republic of China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering
Key Laboratory of Colloid and Interface Chemistry (Shandong University)

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Shandong University, Key Laboratory of Colloid and Interface Chemistry (Shandong University), Ministry of Education, Jinan 250100, People’s Republic of China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A novel luminescence phenomenon of homovanillic acid (HVA) that the excitation wavelength is longer than the emission wavelength (LExL, λex-L > λem) was observed, which was monitored by using a common spectrofluorometer with a xenon lamp as an excitation light source. As the addition of Fe3+, the intrinsic fluorescence of HVA at 310 nm was obviously quenched and a new emission peak at 424 nm emerged and raised under 557 nm excitation. The similar emission spectra of HVA-Fe3+ were also obtained at 287 nm excitation. Thus, a dual-mode ratiometric fluorescence (RF) method for detecting Fe3+ was developed based on LExL and Stokes fluorescence (SF) of HVA. The RF signals of lg(I 424 nm/I 310 nm) had excellent linearity with Fe3+ concentration in the range of 0.50–100 μM. Their detection limits were 0.073 μM and 0.090 μM for the LExL and SF methods, respectively. The LExL and SF methods exhibited high selectivity for detecting Fe3+, and the former has stronger anti-interference ability to Cu2+, Fe2+ etc. The proposed two methods were successfully applied for the determination of Fe3+ in actual water samples. The interaction mechanism of HVA with Fe3+ was also studied.

ce:section.ce:para: Iron ion (Fe3+) plays an important role in biological systems, including oxygen transport, cell metabolism, and enzyme catalysis etc [1]. Free Fe3+ may induce the production of reactive hydroxyl radical, which could severely damage cells, DNA and proteins [2]. And excessive discharge of Fe3+ from industrial processes is potentially harmful for environment and human health. Recently, more researches show that Fe3+ is closely related to neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's disease and Parkinson's disease (PD) [3–5]. Therefore, the development of the method for discrimination and quantitation of Fe3+ is important for environmental monitoring and disease diagnosis. To date, various methods for the determination of Fe3+ have been developed, such as electrochemistry [6], inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry [7], atomic absorption spectroscopy [8] and fluorescent methods [1,2,9]. Especially, the ratiometric fluorescence (RF) method which can effectively reduce interferences from matrix, background noises and temperature compared to single-signal fluorescence has attracted increasing attentions in the detection of Fe3+ [10–15]. Adhikari et al. developed a method for detecting Fe3+ in a RF manner with rhodamine derivatives [16]. Xu et al. adopted the RF of dual-emission carbon dots to detect Fe3+ [17]. These methods all showed good selectivity for Fe3+. Whereas, some reported RF method require complex processes of synthesis and modifying probe, which may be time consuming. Therefore, the development of a simple RF method with multi-signal recognition to quickly detect Fe3+ is still required.

Homovanillic acid (HVA, 4-Hydroxy-3-methoxyphenylacetic acid) is a major metabolite of dopamine [18]. The level of HVA in cerebrospinal fluid correlates positively with the degree of motor impairment in PD patients [19]. When individuals are exposed to excessive Fe3+, the amounts of HVA in the body increase [20]. Therefore, a study on the quantitative analysis of Fe3+ and the interaction between HVA and Fe3+ is helpful in further understanding their roles in the pathogenesis of neurodegenerative diseases. As reported in previous studies, HVA could react with Fe3+ to produce HVA-dimer and Fe3+ also could inhibit HVA enzyme catalysis [21–23]. But the studies are based on the ultraviolet light as excitation. As we know, excitation in the ultraviolet region may lead to more interference to some detection methods. And the single-signal luminescence was adopted, which would limit the selectivity and anti-interference ability of the methods. The longer wavelength excitation and ratiometric fluorescence method could overcome these problems.

Recently, a new type of upconversion photoluminescence (new-UCPL) method was developed by using common spectrofluorometer with a xenon lamp as excitation source [24,25]. Although there are still different viewpoints about the luminescence mechanism, the new-UCPL method has been effectively applied to the photoreduction and the determination of various targets [26–29]. The carbon dots with the new-UCPL characteristic could increase the efficiency in absorbing visible light of the carbon dots-TiO2 nanosheets, thereby promoting photoreduction of Cr(VI) [26]. The downconversion photoluminescence (DCPL) and new-UCPL properties of N-doped carbon nanodots were used for selective and sensitive detection of Cu2+ ions. Compared with the DCPL method, the new-UCPL method had less Fe3+ interference [28].

In this work, we found a novel luminescence phenomenon of HVA solution that the excitation wavelength is longer than the emission wavelength (LExL, λex-L > λem) by using a xenon lamp as the excitation source. The experiment results showed that the LExL of HVA may mainly be assigned to the fluorescence excited by the second-order diffraction light (λex-L/2). Under 557 nm (LExL) or 287 nm (Stokes fluorescence, SF) excitation, the addition of Fe3+ into HVA solution resulted in the intrinsic fluorescence quenching of HVA at 310 nm, while a new fluorescence peak at 424 nm emerged and raised. The changes of I 424 nm/I 310 nm of LExL and SF signals were adopted for detecting Fe3+. The proposed two methods possess high selectivity for Fe3+. Particularly, the LExL method has higher sensitivity and stronger anti-interference ability compared with the SF method. The schematic diagram (Scheme 1 ) displays the interaction mechanism of HVA with Fe3+. The HVA oxidized by Fe3+ and the coordination with Fe3+/Fe2+ mainly caused the fluorescence quenching of HVA at 310 nm, while the formation of HVA-dimer led to the fluorescence enhancement at 424 nm.

Homovanillic acid was commercially obtained from Saan Chemical Technology (Shanghai, China) Co., Ltd. Aqueous solutions of Fe3+, Ba2+, Al3+, Zn2+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Cr3+, Co2+, Ni2+, Cd2+, Pb2+, Hg2+, Ag+, Cu2+, and Fe2+ were made up of FeCl3, BaCl2, AlCl3, ZnSO4, Mg(NO3)2, MnCl2, CrCl3, CoCl2, NiCl2, CdCl2, PbCl2, Hg(NO3)2, AgNO3, CuSO4 and FeCl2, respectively. These inorganic salts (AR) were purchased from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. (Shanghai, China). Citric acid was bought from Aladdin Industrial Co., Ltd. (Shanghai, China). Sodium hydrogen phosphate was purchased from Tianjin Kermel Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. (Tianjin, China). Ultrapure water filtered by 0.22 μm filter membrane (18.25 ΜΩ cm) was used throughout the whole work.

Three-dimensional (3D) fluorescence spectra were conducted on an F-7000 spectrofluorimeter (Hitachi, Japan). The excitation and emission fluorescence spectra were conducted on Fluoromax-4 fluorescence spectrometer (Horiba, Jobin Yvon). Fluorescence decay curves were monitored on an FLS-920 fluorometer (Edinburgh, Britain). UV-vis absorption spectra were measured with a UV-1800 spectrophotometer (Shimadzu, Suzhou). The Fourier transform infrared (FT-IR) spectra were obtained on an Alpha-T spectrophotometer (Bruker, Germany). Freeze drying was carried out on an FDU-1200 freeze dryer (Eyela, Japan). The measurement of pH values was conducted with a pHS-3C acidity meter (Leici, China). The cyclic voltammetric (CV) measurements were carried out on an MPI-EII ECL analyzer (Xi’an Remex Analytical Instrument Co., Ltd., China) using a three-electrode system, including a 5 mm diameter glassy carbon work electrode (GCE), an Ag/AgCl (saturated KCl) reference electrode, and a Pt counter electrode. The photograph was captured in a ZF ultraviolet transmission and reflection analyzer (Shanghai Kanghua Biochemical Instrument Manufacturing Co., Ltd., China).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Collection and pre-treatment of real water samples
ce:section.ce:para: Real water samples were taken from the Daming Lake in Jinan of Shandong province, China. The water samples were filtered through a 0.45 μm polytetrafluoroethylene microporous membrane to remove insoluble particles.

In a 25 mL colorimetric tube, various amounts of Fe3+ solution were added into HVA (1.0 mL, 100 μM) solution. The solutions were mixed and reacted for 8 min at room temperature, followed by adding citric acid-Na2HPO4 buffer solution (1.0 mL, 50 mM), then diluted to 10 mL with water. And after incubation for 5 min, the fluorescence spectra of the mixed solutions were recorded. For the 3D fluorescence spectra, the experimental parameters were set as followed: the photomultiplier voltages: 950 V, scan speed: 30000 nm/min. The slits of excitation and emission: 5 nm. For the excitation and emission fluorescence spectra, the integration time was set as 0.10 s, the excitation and emission slits were 3 nm for SF and 7 nm for LExL, respectively. The quartz cuvette (1 cm × 1 cm) was used. The photograph of aqueous HVA containing various metal ions was captured with UV lamp at 302 nm in an ultraviolet transmission and reflection analyzer.

The fluorescence lifetimes of HVA (100 μM) in the absence and presence of Fe3+ (100 μM) at 310 nm were recorded under 280 nm excitation. And the fluorescence lifetime of HVA-Fe3+ mixture at 424 nm were recorded under 280 nm excitation.

Two mixtures of HVA-Fe3+ were prepared with Fe3+ (1.0 mL 0.010 M) and Fe3+ (0.30 mL 0.10 M) solutions added into HVA (1.0 mL 0.010 M), respectively. After incubating for 8 min, the solutions were transferred respectively to beakers and then were freeze-dried for 12 h at − 40 °C to obtain corresponding solid powders. KBr pressed pellet method was adopted for FT-IR measurement.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence and absorption characteristics of HVA
ce:section.ce:para: The three-dimensional fluorescence spectra of HVA are shown in Fig. 1 a and 1b. It can be seen that the HVA has two fluorescence peaks of the LExL with excitation wavelengths of 460 nm and 557 nm and two peaks of the SF with excitation wavelengths of 230 nm and 280 nm, respectively. And emission peak positions are all the same at 310 nm. We found the excitation wavelengths of LExL (λex-L) are about twice that of SF. To investigate whether the LExL of HVA excited at 557 nm was excited by the second-order diffraction of excitation light (λex-L/2). The fluorescence spectrum of HVA excited at 557 nm was monitored by adding a long-pass filter of 370 nm in the excitation channel to eliminate the effect of second-order diffraction light (557/2 nm). However, the result showed that the LExL of HVA was hardly observed (data not shown). So in this work, the LExL of HVA should mainly be the luminescence excited by the second-order diffraction light (λex-L/2).

The LExL and SF characteristics of HVA depend on the pH conditions of the solution. Here, the effect of pH on the LExL and SF of HVA was studied (Fig. S1). The results demonstrate the LExL and SF of HVA displayed similar pH dependence. In the pH range of 2.2–5.0, the fluorescence intensity of HVA increases significantly with the increasing of pH. The fluorescence intensity of HVA is the highest and remains basically unchanged in pH ranging from 5.0 to 8.0. As the pH was increased continually, the fluorescence intensity of HVA gradually decreases. To further explore the structure of HVA depending on pH, UV-vis spectra were performed under various pH values (Fig. S2). The absorption band of HVA shows two peaks located at 228 nm and 280 nm, which ascribed to the E band and B band of π-π* transition of aromatic sp2 domains of HVA, respectively. In the range of pH 2.5–6.0, the absorbance at 228 nm increases with the increase of pH, the maximum peak shifts slightly to 226 nm and the absorption band is broadened, which results from the dissociation of carboxyl group of HVA [30]. When pH > 7.0, a new peak emerges at 209 nm and the peak at 226 nm becomes a shoulder peak. When the pH value increased to 10, the shoulder peak is red-shifted to 231 nm. In addition, the absorbance at 280 nm keeps basically unchanged in the pH range from 2.5 to 9.0. However, at pH 10, the absorption peak at 280 nm is red-shifted to 300 nm. The change in the absorption profile of HVA at about 280 nm should be caused by the deprotonation of its phenolic hydroxyl group [30,31]. From above results, the highest fluorescence intensity of HVA is mainly attributed to the dissociation of its carboxyl group of HVA rather than the deprotonation of its phenolic hydroxyl group.

The 3D fluorescence spectra of the HVA-Fe3+ system were monitored (Fig. 1c and 1d). It is found that there are four LExL peaks and four SF peaks. Compared with 3D fluorescence spectra of the HVA (Fig. 1a and 1b), both the LExL and SF intensities of HVA-Fe3+ at 310 nm are obviously quenched. And new emission peaks all appear at 424 nm. According to the literatures [21,32,33], the new fluorescence peak at 424 nm should be assigned to the characteristic luminescence of HVA-dimer. We deduced that the HVA-dimer is produced by redox reaction of HVA with Fe3+. The dual-mode RF methods upon the ratio values of the fluorescence intensity at 424 and 310 nm (I 424 nm/I 310 nm) were adopted to detect Fe3+. Furthermore, the LExL spectra of HVA-Fe3+ system have two excitation peaks of located at 500 nm and 570 nm, respectively (see Fig. S3a). Although the LExL intensities at 424 nm excited at 500 nm and 570 nm are higher than that of excited at 557 nm, the LExL intensity at 310 nm is lower. Therefore, the 557 nm was chosen as the excitation wavelength for the further study. When excited at 280 nm, a Raman scattering peak is located at 310 nm (Fig. S3b). To decrease the interference from the Raman scattering, the 287 nm was chosen as excitation wavelength for the SF.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Effects of experimental conditions for the detection of Fe3+
ce:section.ce:para: To obtain the optimal analytical performance, the effects of the order of addition of reagents, types of buffer solutions and reaction/incubation time were investigated (Fig. S4). The results illustrate that the order of addition of reagents has a great impact on the fluorescence intensity of the system. As shown in Fig. S4a, when the buffer solution (pH = 6.00), HVA and Fe3+ solution were added in sequence, only the fluorescence at 310 nm of HVA was quenched. However, when the buffer solution was added after HVA and Fe3+ reaction, not only the intrinsic fluorescence at 310 nm of HVA was drastically quenched but also a new emission peak emerged at 424 nm (Fig. S4a inset). And the fluorescence intensity ratio (I 424 nm/I 310 nm) was the largest under this order of addition of reagent. In addition, the pH value of the 10 μM HVA was about 5.0. And the pH of Fe3+ solution prepared with 0.01 M hydrochloric acid was about 2.0. When Fe3+ was added, the pH of the system was lower than 5.0. The result shows the fluorescence peak at 424 nm is generated under acidic conditions. The above results proved that the redox reaction between HVA and Fe3+ to produce HVA-dimer should occur under acidic conditions. Therefore, the order of reagent addition of HVA, Fe3+ and buffer solution was chosen for the experiment. Considering the addition of different amounts of Fe3+ solution would change the pH of the system. And the result of Fig.S1 shows that the fluorescence intensity of HVA is high and stable when pH is around 6.0–8.0. In order to eliminate the effect of pH changes on the fluorescence intensity of the system and avoid possible influence of Fe3+ hydrolysis, the weak acidic condition with pH = 6.00 ± 0.05 was chosen for the further study. The effects of types of buffer solutions and reaction/incubation time on RF signals were also examined (Figs. S4b and S4c). The experiment results showed that when pH = 6.00, 5.0 mM citric acid-Na2HPO4 buffer solution was added after HVA reacted with Fe3+ for 8 min, and the mixture solution was continued incubating for 5 min, the maximum fluorescence intensity ratio value was obtained.

The selectivity of the two proposed methods for Fe3+ detection was evaluated. The LExL and SF response of HVA to various metal ions was shown in Fig. 2 a. Fluorescence spectra (Fig. 2a inset) demonstrate that no apparent changes in the fluorescence intensity and profile of LExL and SF of HVA are observed with the addition of Ba2+, Al3+, Zn2+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Cr3+, Co2+, Ni2+, Cd2+, Pb2+, Hg2+ and Ag+. Upon addition of Fe2+, Cu2+ and Fe3+ solutions, the fluorescence at 310 nm of HVA was quenched. Among them, only HVA with Fe3+ solution showed the fluorescence at about 424 nm. In addition, photographs of the aqueous HVA containing various metal ions were captured under 302 nm UV lamp (Fig. 2a inset). It can be clearly seen that only HVA with Fe3+ solution emits blue fluorescence. The results show that the interactions between HVA and Fe3+, Fe2+ or Cu2+ are different. We think the blue fluorescence should originate from the HVA-dimer caused by redox reaction between HVA and Fe3+, while the decreases in fluorescence intensity at 310 nm of HVA by Fe2+ and Cu2+ should be attributed to the coordination between them. Although the fluorescence of the HVA at 310 nm can be quenched by Fe2+ and Cu2+, however, it can be seen from Fig. 2a that the changes of I 424 nm/I 310 nm of LExL and SF methods by Fe2+ and Cu2+ are within ± 10%. Other used metal ions also cause little change of fluorescence intensity ratio of LExL and SF (<4%). These results suggest that HVA has excellent selectivity for Fe3+ in RF manner. Besides, the anti-interference performance of the methods was also evaluated. We monitored the fluorescence ratio signals of HVA-Fe3+ mixture in the presence of potential interfering metal ions as equal to the concentration of Fe3+. As shown in Fig. 2b, there is little change in the relative fluorescence intensity ratio value of LExL or SF of HVA-Fe3+ mixture with 100 μM interfering metal ions (< ± 10%). The anti-interference test further proves there is little interference with these RF methods for the determination of Fe3+. Moreover, compared with SF method, the LExL method has less interference with the studied metal ions except Mn2+ and Ag+. Particularly, the LExL method shows significantly stronger anti-interference ability to Fe2+ and Cu2+.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Ratiometric fluorescence for the detection of Fe3+
ce:section.ce:para: To get insight into fluorescence intensity changes at 310 nm and 424 nm with an increase of Fe3+ concentration, the fluorescence spectra of LExL and SF of HVA toward Fe3+ (0–100.0 μM) were monitored under the optimal experimental condition. As shown in Fig. 3 , with the concentration of Fe3+ increases, the fluorescence intensity of HVA at 310 nm gradually decreases, while the fluorescence intensity at 424 nm increases until 20 μM and then decreases. We consider that when the concentration of Fe3+ was low, the redox reaction between Fe3+ and HVA dominated. And then excessive Fe3+ could coordinate with HVA-dimer, causing the quenching of the fluorescence at 424 nm of the system. Both excellent linear relationships were obtained between the logarithm of the fluorescence intensity ratio lg(I 424 nm/I 310 nm) and the logarithm value of Fe3+ concentration in the range from 0.50 μM to 100 μM for LExL method (R2 = 0.9965) and SF method (R2 = 0.9947). The limits of detection (LOD) are 0.073 μM and 0.090 μM for LExL and SF method, respectively (signal-to-noise ratio given as S/N = 3). The linear relationship and the sensitivity of the LExL method are slightly better than that of the SF method. The LODs of LExL and SF methods were both far below the maximum level (5.37 μM) of Fe3+ in drinking water permitted by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency guideline [34]. Compared with some reported RF methods [10–17], as shown in Table 1 , the dual-mode RF methods exhibit comparable LODs and wider linear range for the determination of Fe3+. The proposed methods avoid a time-consuming procedure for synthesis or modifying processes of probe, and these are rapid and simple for the selective detection of Fe3+.

The applicability of the proposed methods was verified, and the standard addition method was adopted for the determination of Fe3+ in actual water samples (Table 2 ). The actual water samples were taken from Daming Lake, Jinan City, Shandong Province. The recovery rates of Fe3+ by the LExL method are in the range of 94.0% − 107.0%, while the recovery rates are 102.0% − 103.3% for SF method. The results suggested that the dual-mode RF method based on HVA has good feasibility for the determination of Fe3+ in actual water samples.

To prove the redox reaction between HVA and Fe3+, cyclic voltammogram curves of HVA and HVA-Fe3+ were recorded (Fig. S5). The redox potential of HVA was at + 0.30 V (vs. SCE). When Fe3+ is added into HVA, the oxidation peak shifts to + 0.60 V. The oxidation potential peak of HVA-Fe3+ is higher than that of HVA and lower than that of Fe3+ (+0.77 V) [35]. The above results inform that there is a redox reaction between HVA and Fe3+. Photographs and UV-vis absorption spectra of HVA-Fe3+ with and without 1,10-phenanthroline (phen) were recorded to further support the redox mechanism (Fig. S6). As shown in Fig. S6 inset, the orange color of the solutions was observed and gradual increases as increasing of Fe3+ concentration. Meanwhile, the absorbance at about 510 nm raised, which means the formation of [Fe(phen)3]2+ complex [36]. It further proved that Fe3+ underwent redox reaction with HVA. The fluorescence lifetimes (τ) of HVA at 310 nm and HVA-Fe3+ at 424 nm were measured. Their lifetimes were 0.80 ns and 2.46 ns, respectively (Figs. S7a and S7b). The large difference in fluorescence lifetimes indicate that the HVA-dimer own longer lifetime than that of HVA.

As above the result, when HVA reacted with Fe3+ in the pH = 6.00 buffer solution, only the fluorescence intensity decrease of HVA at 310 nm was found. We think that there may also be coordination between the HVA and Fe3+, which further quenches the fluorescence at 310 nm. The fluorescence decay curves of HVA-Fe3+ at 310 nm were also recorded by single exponential fitting (Fig. S7c). The τ of HVA-Fe3+ was 0.78 ns (χ 2 = 1.068). The little τ change of HVA with and without Fe3+ suggests HVA-Fe3+ complex formation. Then, the absorption spectra of HVA with various amounts Fe3+ were conducted to further certificate the mechanism (Figs. S8 and 4a). As shown in Fig. S8, the absorbance at 280 nm increases with the increase of the concentration of Fe3+. And Fig. 4 a shows that the absorbance at 280 nm of HVA-Fe3+ is larger than those of the sum value of HVA and Fe3+, which proved the formation of HVA-Fe3+/Fe2+ complex. In addition, there is a significant increase in the absorption band of HVA-Fe3+ system in wavelength range of 300–360 nm, and the absorbance is larger than that of HVA and the sum value of HVA and Fe3+. The changes in absorption profile and absorbance indicate the formation of HVA-dimer [33].

The FT-IR spectra of HVA and HVA-Fe3+ were tested to further investigate the binding of Fe3+ to HVA (shown in Fig. 4b). The peak of HVA at 3105 cm−1 is owing to C–H stretching vibration of its ortho position of hydroxyl group [37]. However, the peak disappears after the addition of Fe3+, which also verifies the HVA-dimer is produced and should be linked by an o,o'-biphenyl linkage. The peak of HVA at 1699 cm−1 is ascribed to stretching vibrations of the C = O of carboxyl group. And the peak at 756 cm−1 and 685 cm−1 correspond to the in plane deformations and out of plane deformations of C = O, respectively [37]. With the addition of Fe3+, those peaks decrease or disappear. Meanwhile, the peaks at 1601 cm−1 and 1409 cm−1 (curves b-c) emerged that are assigned to the antisymmetric stretching vibration (ν as) and symmetric stretching vibration (ν sy) of COO− [38]. The difference value between the ν as and ν sy (Δν = ν as − ν sy) is about 196 cm−1. The Δν value is larger than that of COO− of homovanillic acid sodium salt (161 cm−1) [38], which indicates that the monodentate complex formed between HVA/HVA-dimer and Fe3+/Fe2+ [39]. The peak at 3511 cm−1 and 446 cm−1 is ascribed to the stretching vibrations and the torsion vibrations of phenolic hydroxyl group of HVA, respectively [38]. The two peaks disappeared with the addition of Fe3+, which may be due to the covalent conjugating between Fe3+ with phenolic hydroxyl group of HVA (Interaction mechanism diagram was shown in scheme 1).

Since the large spectral overlap of the absorption spectrum of Fe3+ with the emission spectra of HVA and excitation spectra of SF, inner filter effect (IFE) and fluorescence resonance energy transfer (FRET) should also be considered as the cause of the fluorescence quenching of HVA. Additionally, the approximately identical τ implies that FRET scarcely exists in the system [40]. To further explore whether fluorescence quenching was caused by IFE, the fluorescence intensity was corrected according to the absorbance of the HVA and HVA-Fe3+. The result can be calculated by the following equation: [40–42]
(1)
F
c o r
=
F
o b s d
×
10
(
A
e x
x +
A
e m
y )

Where, F cor is the corrected fluorescence intensity while F obsd is the observed fluorescence intensity via fluorescence measurement; A ex and A em represent the measured absorbance values at the excitation and emission wavelengths, respectively; x and y is the optical path length of excitation and emission beams. Here, the 1 cm × 1 cm path-length cuvette was used (see Fig. S9). Therefore, x and y were 0.5 cm and 0.5 cm, respectively. The IFE parameters of HVA-Fe3+ mixture were shown in Table S1. It is can be seen that the F cor is slightly larger than F obsd. The quenching efficiency come from IFE is within 15%.

In contrast, the LExL method has little IFE because there was almost no spectral overlap between the excitation spectrum and the absorption spectrum of Fe3+, which may lead to the LExL method have higher sensitivity and stronger anti-interference ability than SF method.

In conclusion, the HVA has a novel LExL (λex-L > λem) phenomenon under Xe light excitation. The LExL of HVA may mainly be excited by the second-order diffraction light. Under suitable condition, the interactions of HVA with Fe3+ could cause the fluorescence quenching of HVA at 310 nm and the appearance of a new fluorescence peak at 424 nm, which mainly results from the coordination interaction and redox reaction between them. The LExL and SF ratiometric fluorescence (I 424 nm/I 310 nm) of HVA were both adopted for selectively detecting Fe3+. The methods are promising because of their advantages of simple, fast response, no complicated synthesis processes and wide linear range. The two methods were both successfully applied for the detection of Fe3+ in actual water samples with good recovery efficiency. It is worth mentioning that the LExL method has advantages of higher sensitivity and selectivity than SF method. We expect that this LExL method may provide a new approach for the determination of target analytes.

Hui Zhang: Conceptualization, Methodology, Validation, Formal analysis, Investigation, Data curation, Writing - original draft, Writing - review & editing, Visualization. Xingcen Liu: Writing - review & editing. Jia Sun: Writing - review & editing. Xia Wu: Conceptualization, Methodology, Resources, Writing - review & editing, Supervision, Project administration, Funding acquisition.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was financially supported by Shandong Provincial Natural Science Foundation, China (No. ZR2018MB031) and National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 21545001).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.microc.2020.105051.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
